seoTitle: How to Add a Date in Microsoft Loop (2026)
metaDescription: Insert interactive date chips into Microsoft Loop pages and tables using slash commands so your timelines stay consistent and easy to update.
intro: Typing out dates by hand in different formats leads to inconsistency and typos across a shared page. This guide shows how to insert a live, clickable date chip in Microsoft Loop, both on a plain page and inside a structured table, so your timelines stay accurate and easy to update.

steps.heading: Place your cursor where you want the date
steps.body: Open your target Loop page, move your cursor to the target line, and enter edit mode.

steps.heading: Trigger the date picker
steps.body: Type the /date slash command to instantly bring up the command picker, then select Date from the filtered options.

steps.heading: Or use the plus icon menu
steps.body: Hover over an empty line, click the inline plus toggle, and choose the date component as an alternative way to insert it.

steps.heading: Select your date
steps.body: Click the calendar picker grid to select your specific day, using the arrow controls to switch months as needed.

steps.heading: Add dates inside tables
steps.body: Select a cell in a Loop table and type /date to embed a row-specific date directly inside your tracking grid.

faq.question: Will an inserted date update automatically over time?
faq.answer: No, a date chip represents the specific date you selected, but you can click it at any time to change it to a new date.

faq.question: Can I add dates inside task lists or Kanban boards?
faq.answer: Yes, date chips can be dropped directly inside tracking tables, task lists, and Kanban grids as well as regular text.

faq.question: Do date chips look the same for every collaborator?
faq.answer: Yes, Loop renders live date chips consistently across shared channels so everyone sees the same formatted date.

faq.question: How do I change a date after I've already added it?
faq.answer: Click the existing date chip to reopen the calendar picker and select a new date.
